creator: gebhart@secnetix.de
text:
Ok, summary:
http, https and svn work only with IPv4, but none with IPv6, eg. are probably filtered as all time out.

More detailed:

$ host svn0.eu.freebsd.org.
svn0.eu.freebsd.org is an alias for svnmir.bme.freebsd.org.
svnmir.bme.freebsd.org has address 213.138.116.72
svnmir.bme.freebsd.org has IPv6 address 2001:41c8:112:8300::e6a:0
svnmir.bme.freebsd.org mail is handled by 0 .
$ ping -c 3 svn0.eu.freebsd.org. 
PING svnmir.bme.freebsd.org (213.138.116.72): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 213.138.116.72: icmp_seq=0 ttl=49 time=29.816 ms
64 bytes from 213.138.116.72: icmp_seq=1 ttl=49 time=29.708 ms
64 bytes from 213.138.116.72: icmp_seq=2 ttl=49 time=29.769 ms

--- svnmir.bme.freebsd.org ping statistics ---
3 packets transmitted, 3 packets received, 0.0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max/stddev = 29.708/29.764/29.816/0.044 ms
$ ping6 -c 3 svn0.eu.freebsd.org. 
PING6(56=40+8+8 bytes) 2001:a60:9026::85 --> 2001:41c8:112:8300::e6a:0
16 bytes from 2001:41c8:112:8300::e6a:0, icmp_seq=0 hlim=46 time=26.810 ms
16 bytes from 2001:41c8:112:8300::e6a:0, icmp_seq=1 hlim=46 time=26.698 ms
16 bytes from 2001:41c8:112:8300::e6a:0, icmp_seq=2 hlim=46 time=26.588 ms

--- svnmir.bme.freebsd.org ping6 statistics ---
3 packets transmitted, 3 packets received, 0.0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max/std-dev = 26.588/26.699/26.810/0.091 ms
#
# so far, so good
#
$ telnet svn0.eu.freebsd.org. svn
Trying 2001:41c8:112:8300::e6a:0...
telnet: connect to address 2001:41c8:112:8300::e6a:0: Operation timed out
Trying 213.138.116.72...
Connected to svnmir.bme.freebsd.org.
# same for port http and https
# telnet and most other programs connect to IPv4 if IPv6 doesn't work, but svn doesn't and so:
$ svn co https://svn0.eu.freebsd.org/base/releng/9.3 src
svn: E000060: Unable to connect to a repository at URL 'https://svn0.eu.freebsd.org/base/releng/9.3'
svn: E000060: Error running context: Operation timed out

I hope you can see the problem now.
